creator: gbell72@rogers.com
text:
--- Tijl Coosemans <tijl@ulyssis.org> wrote:

> Can you try "patch-wine" at <http://wiki.freebsd.org/Wine>?
> 

I rebuilt world and kernel with the 7.0-CURRENT patches and can now
successfully create the ~/.wine directory.  Running winecfg still shows
the unhandled page faults though.  Backtrace below.

dan@home$ winedbg winecfg
wine: creating configuration directory '/home/dan/.wine'...
wine: Unhandled page fault on write access to 0x7e1ad804 at address
0x7e16b922 (thread 0009), starting debugger...
file_set_error: Bad address
file_set_error: Bad address
wine: '/home/dan/.wine' created successfully.
WineDbg starting on pid 000a
file_set_error: Bad address
file_set_error: Bad address
0x7e364966: subl        $12,%esp
Backtrace:
=>1 0x7e364966 in kernel32 (+0x44966) (0x0035ffe8)

Register dump:

 EIP:7e364966 ESP:0035ff80 EBP:0035ffe8 EFLAGS:00200202(   - 00      -
- I1)

 ESI:00110000 EDI:7e42f26c
